e64_context_policy.context_derivation: V67 E64 uses sequence plus public non-coordinate metadata. Explicit transmembrane/topology-provider evidence may initialize membrane grammar; hydrophobicity-only and generic membrane mentions do not. Generic 'complex' wording is not oligomer evidence by itself.
reasons: public non-coordinate component list contains biological cofactor candidates: ['CA']
public title/description/keywords mention ligand or cofactor context
E64 ignores generic 'complex' wording unless copy/assembly/oligomer evidence is present

prediction_sources.evidence_statement: Raw amino-acid sequence for the RCSB polymer entity.

RCSB title: CGTASE FROM THERMOANAEROBACTERIUM THERMOSULFURIGENES EM1 IN COMPLEX WITH A MALTOHEXAOSE INHIBITOR. Entity description: CYCLODEXTRIN GLYCOSYLTRANSFERASE. Organism: Thermoanaerobacterium thermosulfurigenes. Polymer composition: protein/oligosaccharide. Sequence-derived marks: sequence-derived low-complexity tendency high; sequence-derived hydrophobic closure tendency; generic complex wording withheld from oligomer evidence. Coordinates, native contacts, residue-residue distances, ligand geometry, and validation annotations are unopened before the prediction hash.

V67 E64 metadata context marks: cofactor_context ligand_context metal_context. E64 uses explicit transmembrane/topology evidence and ignores generic complex wording as oligomer evidence. generic complex alone is not assembly_context. Coordinates, contacts, ligand geometry, native topology, and post-seal validation labels are blocked before sealing.
